subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, CRS, ACLED) + Kenyan official (KNBS, Central Bank of Kenya, Treasury) + Kenyan independent press (Daily Nation, The Standard, The Star).
Politics: a government strained after the deadly June-2024 Gen-Z anti-tax protests and 2025 unrest; democratic but pressured, with a 2027 election ahead.

Standout features: credible monetary management, well-anchored inflation, and a strong constitutional/judicial framework, against high debt, climate exposure, and 2024-25 repression/cohesion strain. Source-lean: HIGH but low-distortion Anglophone (English is official).

economic.notes: A credible central bank, well-anchored inflation, and sound banks, against a heavy debt overhang.

fiscal_state.notes: High debt ~68% GDP and heavy debt-service, with the 2024 finance-bill protests forcing tax U-turns that deepened the fiscal hole and IMF-program friction. (IMF; World Bank; Treasury)

monetary_independence.notes: The central bank is credible, with the shilling steady since 2024 and reserves rebuilt. (CBK)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The shilling is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Within the target band and well-managed. (CBK)

banking_stability.notes: A sophisticated, well-capitalised regional banking sector, with some sovereign-exposure risk. (CBK; World Bank)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Diversified - agriculture/tea/horticulture plus a services/fintech hub - with a persistent trade deficit and no resource curse. (World Bank; KNBS)

institutional.notes: A strong constitution and credible statistics, against entrenched corruption and impunity.

rule_of_law.notes: An assertive judiciary and a strong 2010 constitution, against entrenched corruption, impunity, and alleged 2024-25 extrajudicial killings. (WJP; TI; KHRC)

statistical_integrity.notes: The statistics agency is credible, with some public-debt-data gaps flagged. (KNBS; IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Functional but uneven, with devolution-era capacity gaps and weak revenue mobilisation. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: A vibrant, pluralistic press, against 2024-25 abduction and digital-intimidation concerns. (RSF; Amnesty)

political_social.notes: A demographic dividend, against protest-era repression, ethnic-bloc politics, and youth unemployment.

political_stability.notes: The government is strained after the deadly June-2024 anti-tax protests and 2025 unrest; democratic but pressured, with a 2027 election ahead. (Amnesty; CFR)

civil_liberties.notes: Constitutionally strong but undercut by protest crackdowns, abductions, and online-speech reprisals. (Amnesty; KHRC)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful work/residence routes and an expat hub, hosting large refugee camps under encampment constraints. (UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: Ethnic-bloc politics, high inequality, and acute youth unemployment driving unrest. (World Bank)

demographics.notes: A young, fast-growing population - a dividend strained by a jobs gap. (KNBS; UN)

geopolitical.notes: A valued Western partner and regional hub, against cross-border terrorism exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: A US/Western partner (a major non-NATO ally) and African diplomatic hub - pragmatic. (CRS)

conflict_involvement.notes: Cross-border al-Shabaab terrorism from Somalia and a Haiti-mission entanglement, with no interstate war. (ACLED; ICG)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Broadly open and unsanctioned, with some FX friction. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Good regional infrastructure, against drought/flood exposure, an underfunded health system, and elevated crime.

climate.notes: Horn drought plus deadly 2024 floods on a rain-dependent agriculture, with relatively better regional readiness. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: A universal-health push but underfunded and uneven, with recurrent doctor strikes. (World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Good by regional standards - roads, a railway, strong mobile/digital - but debt-financed. (World Bank; KNBS)

crime_safety.notes: Urban crime and terrorism-adjacent risk in border and coastal zones. (CRS)

disaster_insurance.notes: Very low penetration, with some parametric drought schemes. (World Bank)

summary: A regional economic hub with genuine strengths - a credible central bank, well-anchored inflation, a sophisticated banking sector, a strong 2010 constitution and assertive judiciary, a vibrant press, and a fintech-led services economy - set against a heavy debt overhang, entrenched corruption, and the strain of the deadly 2024 anti-tax protests and 2025 unrest. Climate exposure (drought and flood), an underfunded health system, elevated urban and border-zone crime, and ethnic-bloc politics weigh on the other side, leaving a genuinely two-sided profile.
